promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: CACNA1E encodes a somatosensory gene tuning touch, pain, itch, temperature, mechanosensation, proprioception, or interoceptive sensory signaling. This topic bundle emphasizes human sensory biology, with interpretation centered on visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local CACNA1E bundle is oriented toward human sensory biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: CACNA1E variants can alter, tag, or contextualize visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Human senses, sensory transduction, and sensory-neuron research context
Vision, hearing, balance, smell, taste, touch, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoception pathway biology
Candidate-gene triage where sensory organ, receptor class, channel gating, tissue expression, and phenotype timing change interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: CACNA1E methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for CACNA1E.
Because CACNA1E biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use CACNA1E promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CACNA1E human sensory biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.
